name: Yaroslavl Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the golden field, recalling the wealth of the Yaroslavl land and its historic icon-painting tradition
the rampant bear, the historic emblem of Yaroslavl
the silver poleaxe held by the bear
the bear's tongue and details of the coat of arms

description: A golden-yellow field bearing in the centre a black bear standing on its hind legs and shouldering a silver poleaxe, the historic emblem of Yaroslavl.
history: Adopted on 1 June 2010 by Law of Yaroslavl Oblast No. 18-z; the central charge is taken from the historic Yaroslavl coat of arms granted in 1778, which itself reflects the foundational legend of Prince Yaroslav the Wise.

features.symbolism: A golden-yellow field charged at the centre with the historic Yaroslavl bear holding a silver poleaxe

a black bear standing erect on its hind legs and holding a silver poleaxe over its left shoulder; according to legend, Yaroslav the Wise (founder of Yaroslavl in 1010) slew a bear at the site of the city, and the bear has been the city's emblem ever since
